chapter_context.summary: Two spies are sent into Jericho and are hidden by Rahab, who expresses faith in God.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rahab Hides the Spies (2:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on Rahab's Faith (2:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on The Escape and Report (2:15-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.

key_themes.title: Courage
key_themes.description: The requirement to be 'strong and courageous' in the face of giants.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness
key_themes.description: God's fulfillment of His promises to give Israel the land.

devotional.question: What 'Jericho walls' in your life are you trusting God to bring down?
How can you build 'memorial stones' to remember God's victories in your journey?

cultural_historical.title: The Conquest
cultural_historical.text: The book of Joshua records the transition from a wandering nation to a settled one, fulfilling the patriarchal promises.
